name: PaperDebugger
what_it_does: PaperDebugger is a Chrome extension and multi-agent backend system that integrates with Overleaf to provide AI-assisted academic writing critique, revision suggestions, comment generation, and literature research directly within a LaTeX editing session.
use_cases: Getting AI critique and revision suggestions on a LaTeX paper open in Overleaf without leaving the editor
Generating automated reviewer-style comments and inserting them directly into the Overleaf project
Running literature research and citation verification agents on paper content
Using custom prompt templates for domain-specific academic editing and proofreading
not_for: Non-Overleaf LaTeX workflows (offline TeX editors, other platforms)
Users needing the AI to directly modify or commit changes to the document
best_when: A researcher actively writing a paper in Overleaf wants inline AI critique and literature cross-checking without switching to a separate AI chat window.
avoid_when: The team uses offline LaTeX tools, or needs AI that can directly edit and push changes to the document rather than read-only suggestions.

auth.notes: JWT-based authentication with OAuth support. Backend uses Go/Gin with MongoDB. Self-hosted backend URL configurable in extension.

pricing.notes: AGPL-3.0 license. Underlying OpenAI API costs apply for AI calls. Chrome extension free to install.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC-based backend; error messages depend on Gin/gRPC propagation to Chrome extension.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only access to Overleaf content; critique generation is idempotent in effect if not in output.
known_agent_gotchas: No MCP server; AI agents cannot integrate directly without the Chrome extension
Requires Chrome browser and Overleaf session; not usable in headless environments
Self-hosted backend requires Go 1.24+, MongoDB, and OpenAI API key configuration
AGPL-3.0 license has copyleft implications for commercial derivatives

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
